title: Okay, One Actual Last One (Diary Cleanup Day)

sentences.en: Thursday. I told Bernice I was finally done writing catch-up entries, and she said, "Sure, okay," in a tone that clearly did not believe me.
sentences.zh: 星期四。我跟 Bernice 說我終於寫完補寫日記了，她說「好啊，喔好」，語氣顯然完全不相信我。

sentences.en: While cleaning my desk, I found an old note from a classmate's absence excuse, still pinned with a small pin to my old bulletin board.
sentences.zh: 整理書桌的時候，我發現一張同學的請假單舊筆記，還用一根小別針釘在我舊的公告板上。

sentences.en: I never mentioned that our school held a brief fire drill this year, teaching us the correct exit and reminding everyone to stay calm, not run in a panic.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過，學校今年辦過一次簡短的消防演習，教我們正確的出口，並提醒大家要保持冷靜，不要驚慌亂跑。

sentences.en: There was a science lesson I forgot to describe, explaining how heat energy causes molecules to move faster, which honestly explains why my room feels awful in summer.
sentences.zh: 有一堂理化課我忘了描述，解釋熱能如何讓分子移動得更快，老實說，這也說明了為什麼我房間夏天感覺這麼糟。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that William, in a rare moment of leadership, organized his friends into a small cleanup team for the classroom fish tank.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，William 難得展現領導能力，把朋友組織成一支小小的清潔隊，負責整理教室的魚缸。

sentences.en: There was an evening I never described when Dad tried to fix the sink himself, and by the end, we needed a professional anyway, at a fairly reasonable fee.
sentences.zh: 有個晚上我從沒描述過，爸爸想自己修水槽，結果最後我們還是得找專業的來，收費倒是相當合理。

sentences.en: I never mentioned a classmate who moved away this year, an emotional goodbye that reminded the whole class how quickly a school year can change.
sentences.zh: 我從沒提過一位今年搬走的同學，那次充滿情緒的道別，提醒了全班一個學年可以變化得多快。

sentences.en: There was a small argument I forgot to record: Bernice and I could not agree on the correct length for our joint history project, until we finally compromised.
sentences.zh: 有場小爭執我忘了記下來：Bernice 和我對我們共同歷史報告的正確長度談不攏，最後才終於妥協。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our teacher gave a short introduction to basic economics, explaining supply and demand using the price of school cafeteria snacks as an example.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，我們老師簡短介紹了基本經濟學，用學校餐廳零食的價格當例子，解釋供給和需求。

sentences.en: There was a quiet afternoon I never described: Sophia sorting old photographs into piles, deciding which ones deserved a permanent place in the family album.
sentences.zh: 有個安靜的下午我從沒描述過：Sophia 把舊照片分類成一堆一堆，決定哪些值得永久收進家庭相簿裡。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention that our class watched a short documentary about survival in extreme environments, which made our own ordinary daily complaints feel a little smaller.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提，我們班看了一部關於在極端環境中求生的短片，這讓我們平常那些日常抱怨感覺渺小了一些。

sentences.en: There was a moment I never wrote about: William trying, and failing completely, to whistle, producing mostly just air and disappointment.
sentences.zh: 有個時刻我從沒寫過：William 嘗試吹口哨，卻徹底失敗，發出來的大多只是氣音和失望。

sentences.en: I forgot to mention the sample essay our teacher shared, praising its clear structure and honest emotion as an ideal example for the rest of us to study.
sentences.zh: 我忘了提老師分享的一篇範文，稱讚它結構清晰、情感誠實，是我們其他人學習的理想範例。

sentences.en: Lesson of this genuinely, truly last catch-up entry: some stories only become clear once you look back at an entire year all at once, not one single day at a time.
sentences.zh: 這篇真正、完全最後一篇補寫日記的心得：有些故事只有把一整年一次看完，而不是一天一天分開看，才會真正變得清楚。

quiz.question: What does the school fire drill teach students?
options: The correct exit and to stay calm instead of running in a panic.
How to use a fire extinguisher.

quiz.question: What example does the teacher use to explain supply and demand?
options: The price of school cafeteria snacks.

quiz.question: What is Sophia doing on the quiet afternoon Coco describes?
options: Sorting old photographs into piles for the family album.
